title: Contact
description: A named person attached to a customer or a specific site. Replaces eq-solves-service's two parallel tables (customer_contacts, site_contacts) with a single canonical entity where exactly one of customer_id / site_id is set. Site-level contacts are surfaced on site reports; customer-level contacts are surfaced on customer dashboards.

customer_id.description: Customer this contact belongs to. Exactly one of customer_id / site_id is set.

site_id.description: Site this contact belongs to. Exactly one of customer_id / site_id is set.

role.description: Free-text job title or role (e.g. 'Facilities Manager', 'Site Supervisor', 'Procurement').

phone.description: AU phone, coerced to standard format.

is_primary.description: Whether this is the primary contact for the parent (customer or site). At most one primary per parent.

active.description: Whether this contact is still current.

x-eq-cross-field-rules.rule: (customer_id != null AND site_id == null) OR (customer_id == null AND site_id != null)
x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: A contact must belong to exactly one of customer_id or site_id.
Contact has neither email nor phone — unlikely to be useful.

x-eq-import-hints.description: Contact lists usually arrive as a separate sheet appended to a customer or site import. Watch for one-row-per-site contacts collapsed into a single 'Site Contact' column on the site sheet — those need promotion into this entity.

common_sources.name: Customer contact sheet
watchouts: Primary often blank — first contact per customer defaults to primary

common_sources.name: Inline site contact on a site register
watchouts: Extract into contact rows with site_id set; leave site.site_contact_* as the cached primary
